observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to: 1. recognize depreciation periodically for all depreciable assets; 2. reclassify all completed projects still carried in the Construction in Progress accounts to the appropriate asset accounts based on the Certificates of Completion issued by the Office of the Municipal Engineer; and 3. prepare adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies noted. Moreover, we recommended that the Property Custodian to: 1. conduct complete inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and 2. continuously reconcile its records with the Office of the Municipal Accountant. 3. We also recommended that the management initiate the necessary procedures to cleanse its PPE account to achieve the fair presentation of the account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report. Incomplete reconciliation of records between the accounting and supply officer records was still observed. Management has not yet initiated procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant commence with the reconciliation of the undocumented balance of the Due to NGAs account; and 2. henceforth comply with the pertinent provisions of the IPSAS 1 by recording the transactions in adherence with the requirement of fair presentation of accounts and adequately disclosing relevant information in the Notes to FS for these to be fairly presented.
observations.reason: Due to NGA account was not disclosed in the Notes to FS. Due to NGA account balance in adherence with the requirement of fair presentation was not adequately disclosed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant strictly follow the disclosure requirements in Item 47 of IPSAS 24 and Annex G, Note 33 of COA Circular 2016-004.
observations.reason: There is no reconciliation disclosure on the balance in SCBAA and Statement of Financial Performance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as head of the Municipal Development Council and all LGU personnel concerned to: 1. appropriate proper planning in identifying priority development projects to ensure that PPAs are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready as mandated by laws and regulations; and 2. appropriate sufficient funds for implementation of the projects to be constructed in phases, thereby ensuring that each completed phase can be utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.reason: Two projects were still unimplemented due to the insufficiency of preparation to ensure the project was well-planned. Some actions were implemented while others remain not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the OIC-MDRRMO includes all the unexpended prior years’ balances in the LDRRMFIP to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness for natural and man-made hazards or disasters.
observations.reason: Prior years’ balances are still not included in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to reconcile the inventory records, set up and maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards for all PPE items; the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation periodically for all depreciable assets; and the officers concerned to observe COA Circular No. 2015-008 so that proper disclosures of the road network account in the FS will be achieved.
observations.reason: Road Network account was not properly disclosed. The property cards for many items were not submitted; disclosure and reconciliations remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a complete subsidiary ledger for every fund transfer and adequately disclose in the face of the Statement of Financial Position or in the Notes the necessary details of the Due to NGAs account to provide users with additional information that is useful in relevant decision-making. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to evaluate and review the existing fund balances in order to determine the proper disposition of the funds and return the excess fund to the source agencies.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers were not submitted to the Audit Team. Due to NGAs balance was not disclosed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local Development Council, spearhead: 1. appropriate planning of priority development projects including the indication of the expected start and completion dates; 2. monitoring of the implementation of the priority projects; and 3. optimal utilization of the 20% DF in order to promptly deliver its benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. No adequate planning and monitoring undertaken; projects remain unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality through the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare a separate budget, maintain a separate special account, and prepare a complete set of Financial Statements for the MWSS to provide adequate information on the financial sustainability of the public utility.
observations.reason: Transactions for the Water System are still recorded under the General Fund of the LGU; separate accounting and reports have not been prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate appropriate legal actions to recover from officials and employees who were already separated from service and/or file a request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off receivables that are deemed uncollectible, after all remedies have been exhausted.
observations.reason: For request of write-off; no legal actions reported.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required physical count of all properties of the Municipality, prepare complete and detailed RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team. Moreover, to reconcile the disparity between the book balance and the inventory report listing in order to ascertain the correctness and reliability of the reported balances. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supply Ledger Card, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card, Property Card, while the Municipal Treasurer should also maintain Stock Card and Property Card, he should also prepare and submit to accounting unit the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS).
observations.reason: The Supply Officer did not submit property cards for all properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Officials concerned to exert effort in identifying the details or schedule of these aforementioned accounts to fairly present the balances on the financial statements.
observations.reason: There is still no submission of the details or schedule of the aforementioned accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly and quarterly trial balance, financial statements and its accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by the above rules and regulations in order to have a timely verification and analysis of accounts and the immediate submission of the audit results to management to have a reliable information on the financial condition and results of operations of the municipality.
observations.reason: There is no submission of monthly and quarterly financial statements for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their Cash Local Treasury account balances to ensure the reliability of the balances of Cash Local Treasury account reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant:
a. In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to verify and validate the dormant accounts’ collectibility status; and
b. To facilitate the completion of the necessary documents and request an authority from the Commission on Audit (COA) to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱2,440,638.49.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventories semi-annually and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to ensure that the accounting and property records are reconciled.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to expedite the reconciliation of their records for PPE accounts and make necessary adjustments taking into consideration the procedural guidelines for one-time cleansing of PPEs provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Consider appointing a GSO, who will be primarily in charge in the conduct periodic physical inventory of all PPE of the LGU-Mercedes.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all amounts withheld from the salaries of LGU officials and employees due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth be remitted in full within the prescribed period to prevent under remittances.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to immediately remit all overdue taxes withheld and conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to ensure that the exact and accurate amounts are remitted to the BIR to avoid deficiencies.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
a. Concerned official to monitor the status of transferred funds to identify unimplemented projects or unutilized balances of completed PPAs and comply with COA Circular No. 94-013 on the return of the unexpended balances in a timely manner; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Bureau of Treasury on how to return the unexpended balances with unidentified or closed SA.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
a. Implement all pending development projects targeted under the 20 per cent LDF for CY 2024; and
b. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent LDF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready by continuously pursuing improved practices in planning, programming and implementing programs and projects.

We reiterate the previous audit recommendation that the LCE to facilitate the establishment of an standard operating procedures in a form of a local policy to establish clear guidelines on the selection, hiring, and daily rates of JO personnel.
We recommended that the LCE require the Local DRRM Officer to monitor the implementation of PPAs from continuing LDRRMF, and the STF to facilitate prompt completion.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to ensure timely submission of the GPB and GAD AR to Audit Team in accordance with the provision of Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, to timely monitor and evaluate the utilization of GAD Fund.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Administrator to:
a. Ensure the utilization of the required format for the Report of the Fuel Consumption to facilitate the evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumed during a specified period; and
b. Instruct all drivers of the LGU-Mercedes to ensure that the DTTs are properly accomplished before submission or attaching them to disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. Stop the practice of giving fuel to privately owned motor vehicles;
b. If private vehicle was used during the official travel of a government official/employee, reimburse the equivalent cost of customary mode of transportation to the latter; and
c. Revisit the Budget and the available funds of LGU-Mercedes to augment the funds for additional service vehicles for its officials/employees and constituents.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Observe prompt submission of DVs and ORs including the corresponding reports each month on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month; and
Strictly adhere to the cited rules and regulations on the rendition of accounts and reports be enforced by the concerned officials in order not to incur penalty as provided in Section 122 (2) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a complete monthly bank reconciliation statement for each bank
account and effect correction and adjustment of the accounts in order to present fairly
in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Inventory Committee to
conduct the required physical count of all properties of the Municipality, prepare the
RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team. Also, for the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the disparity between the book balance and the inventory report listing in
order to ascertain the correctness and reliability of the reported balances.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts in the books for all funds and effect
adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to carefully review the account titles to be used in recording the financial
transactions of the Municipality. Moreover, for the Municipal Treasurer to update its
real property tax account register and provide the Municipal Accountant, at the
beginning of each year, a certified list of the taxpayers as well as its total amount due
and collected for the recording of the proper receivable account by the Municipal
Accountant.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the correctness of procedure in the recording of all expense
accounts for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We, therefore, recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Municipal Officials concerned to exert effort in identifying the details or schedule of
these accounts to fairly present the balances on the financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the amounts presented on the face of the
financial statements and its corresponding notes to financial statements to fairly
present the financial condition and performance of the Municipality and to avoid
confusion to the users of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall henceforth, strictly require
the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete set of financial statements on or
before February 14 of each year.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to provide reasonable basis for recording the RPT and SET Receivable in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection activities against these delinquent real property owners, giving them final grace period after which to initiate appropriate sanctions and available legal remedies in order to enforce compliance.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the monthly and quarterly trial balance, financial
statements and its accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by the
above rules and regulations in order to have a timely verification and analysis of
accounts and the immediate submission of the audit results to management to
have a reliable information on the financial condition and results of operations of
the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal
Accountant to properly compute and record the depreciation of all depreciable
assets of the Municipality in accordance with the applicable standards and make
the necessary adjusting entries in order to present fairly the accounts in the
financial statements. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to make
adjusting entries on PPE accounts recognized in Trust Fund which should be in
the General Fund.

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Engineer, General Services Officer/Property Custodian and Municipal
Accountant in the implementation of COA Circular No. 2015-008 in relation to
Road Networks recognition in the book of accounts of the Agency.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to
record properly the cash transactions in the cashbooks to ascertain correct
balances and maintain a complete set of cashbooks. We also recommend to
direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all cash collection to the depository
bank within the period as specified in the above mentioned regulation in order
to properly safeguard government funds from misuse or loss.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer must take proper procedures in
ascertaining the identity of the social pensioners' representative in relation to the
disbursement of fund on their behalf in accordance with existing rules and
regulations in order to verify the propriety of the claim. Moreover, attach the
necessary supporting documents to support the disbursement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the Bids and Award
Committee (BAC) and Municipal Engineer to promptly implement the priority
development projects as approved by the Local Development Council and
stipulated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for CY 2018 in order to optimally
attain the desirable outcomes in terms of socio-economic and environmental
development of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Officer in the strict implementation of the
programs, projects and activities in accordance with the approved LDRRMF
Plan in order to attain the policy of the state to uphold the people’s constitutional
rights to life and property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive allocate funds necessary in the
carrying-out of prerequisite activities and technical training to facilitate the
creation of the Local Solid Waste Management Plan in order that the
Municipality can contribute to the overall attainment of the policy of the state
regarding systematic and ecological solid waste management program.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to exert effort in the enforcement of collection of monthly rental of
stallholders and imposition of penalty in order to augment its collection for the
operation of the municipality.

Moreover, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive to re-visit the market
operation with a proposal to amend or upgrade the revenue code of the
municipality taking into consideration the cost and the benefits derived thereat.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the submission of
identification documents of those personnel who did not appear during the
physical count. Moreover, in coordination with HRMO, the Local Chief
Executive must create a stringent procedure of hiring contractual workers in
order to ascertain their identity and to validly support any disbursement arising
therefrom.

We recommended to the Chief Executive to consider the necessity of work/job in
hiring job order personnel in order to prevent redundancy of work.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive and to the concerned personnel to
exercise prudent and stop payment of traveling expenses of the contractual and
job order workers so that funds can be used for development programs and
projects of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the GAD Focal
Person to maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of
GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objective of the
program to pursue women's empowerment and gender equality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management limit the appropriations and expenditures for
personal services to 55% of the total income from regular sources of the next
preceding fiscal year in order not to prejudice the priority needs of the
municipality as a whole.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly
bank reconciliation statements for all funds maintained for Cash in Bank in
pursuance to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 to 3.4 of COA Circular No.
96-011 in order to present a reliable and accurate balance of the account.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the said disbursement vouchers and payrolls as well as the
original copies of cancelled checks and checks not reported in the RCI in
accordance with the provision specified in Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-
006 dated September 15, 2006.

We recommend that the management strictly comply Section 3 of COA
Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the utilization of the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund to further strengthen the LGU’s
capacity for Disaster Risk Reduction Management. Likewise prevent the
suspension or disallowance of pertinent transactions in the future audit.

We recommend that the management strictly comply with Section 5.1.5 of COA
Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to further strengthen the capabilities of the
LGU in addressing Disaster Risk Reduction Management through regular
monitoring of its PPAs.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create the Local GAD Focal Point
System in order to implement effectively and efficiently the local GAD
programs, projects and activities, ensure alignment with the guidelines on the
localization of the Magna Carta of Women (MCW) and to address the gender
issues and concerns in the locality which are beneficial to both the LGU clientele
and the organization.

Moreover, prepare and submit approved GAD plan and budget, 2015 GAD
Accomplishment Report, GAD Code and Database to the DILG for review and
evaluation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through its Solid Waste Management
Board comply strictly all the provisions of the Republic Act No. 9003 or
otherwise known as the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to achieve
the intended purpose of the law and to ensure protection of public health and
environment and eliminate against air, water and chemicals pollution as well as
ecological imbalance.

Moreover, prepare and submit the revised and corrected Local Solid Waste
Management Plans (10-year) as recommended by the DENR-EMB for approval
by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make representation
with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste
Management Programs, Projects and Activities based on the revised and
approved SWM Plans for immediate and proper implementation.

We recommend that management comply strictly the provisions of DILG-DBM
Circular No. 2011-1 in order that appropriated funds will be utilized effectively
and efficiently. Moreover, submit regularly Report of Utilization for review and
monitoring purposes.

We recommend that management fast-tract or speed-up the implementation of all
the programmed projects and activities charged to 20% EDF during the year to
achieve effectively and efficiently its socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Management:

a. Stop the granting of cash advances to officers, who are neither accountable
officers nor employees of the Municipality, such as PSDS and School
Principals, in conformity with rules and regulations;

b. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances in the
books as of December 31, 2016 amounting to F235,000.00; and

observations.reason: Bank Statement on Statement has not been submitted.

observations.recommendation: Serve demand letters to those officials and employees, requiring them to settle or liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances; Stop the practice of granting cash advances for travels of officials and employees with outstanding and unliquidated cash advances; Strictly enforce the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 after the lapsed of thirty (30) days from service of demand letters and implement monitoring and subsidiary ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: AA Implemented. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger cards for each official/employee.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget and the GAD Accomplishments immediately to the Audit Team for purposes.
observations.managementAction: AA R, Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: AA R, Partially Implemented. The Solid Waste Program is on-going.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the AA Municipal Accountant – Designate to exert effort in the preparation and submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements in order to present a reliable balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account.
observations.managementAction: Municipal R, Accountant – Designate to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements to correct the Cash in Bank account.
observations.reason: Bank reconciliation statement has not been submitted as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: Serve demand letters to those officials and employees requiring them to settle or liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances; Stop the practice of granting cash advances for travels of officials and employees with outstanding and unliquidated cash advances; Instruct OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger cards for monitoring; Implement Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 after the lapsed of thirty (30) days from service of demand letters.
observations.managementAction: AA Implemented.

observations.recommendation: Instruct all concerned LGU officials to formulate and prepare the GAD Plan and Budget to insure that funds are allocated for Gender and Development programs/projects; allocate at least 5% of their budget for CY 2012.
observations.managementAction: AA R, Implemented. The Municipal Budget Officer and other concerned officials to abide with circular on integration of GAD.

observations.recommendation: Prepare the annual procurement plan and conduct public bidding in all of their procurement, except in specific highly exceptional cases under which procurement may be done through the alternative method.
observations.managementAction: AA R, Implemented. Procurement plan prepared.

observations.recommendation: Procure common-use supplies only at the PS Depot, as mandated by AO No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: AA R, Implemented. Certification from the PS Depot is attached to the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: Require the AA that Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Card be maintained for each category of property and land, respectively. Further, these properties should be registered in the name of the municipality with the Register of Deeds and duly supported with proof of ownership such as the certificate of land title.
observations.managementAction: R, Implemented. Documents attached to the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: Require the AA Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements to correct the Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: Municipal R, Accountant prepared and submitted the aforementioned financial reports and statements; submit written explanation for the unjustified failure to submit the financial reports and statements on time; and submit financial reports within reglementary period set by the Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the: ¢ Municipal Journal Entry Accountant to record all valid reconciling items and investigate for the variance to achieve an accurate financial reporting and correct the balance of Cash in Bank. *  Municipal Treasurer to provide some necessary data regarding those unadjusted book reconciling items and variances, if any, to facilitate the required adjustment and correction in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Accountant prepared Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 100-160102904, 100-160102908, 100-16010290C, 100-16010290E 100-17020677 to record $23,838.95 variance to correct the balance of Cash in Bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: e Stop the granting of the cash advance to Mr. Noniel R. Evia in excess of the amount covered by his fidelity bond; or e Otherwise, require the Municipal Treasurer to increase the bond of the said disbursing officer enough to cover his accountability to ensure adequate protection of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendations. During CY 2016, The Municipality distributed the salaries of all personnel including contractual and job orders employees through Automated Teller Machine (ATMs) instead of establishing payroll fund for the disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete records, property ledger cards and stocks cards, respectively, and intensify review and reconciliation of those records to come up with reconciled balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Management concerned promised to hire additional personnel who will conduct physical count and maintain property records. This observation was reiterated in AAR for CY 2016.
observations.reason: offices are lacking of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct: & All officials observe the proper utilization of specific-purpose allowances particularly the RATA; and e The Municipal Accountant to prohibit loan amortizations charged to RATA, considering that the purpose of said allowance is to defray representation and transportation expenses in accordance with DBM LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 All officials complied to observe the proper utilization of RATA starting April, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should not allow employees under JO contracts to attend trainings and seminar and claim travelling expenses, to prevent the incurrence of additional maintenance and other operating expenses. Likewise, ensure that the DTRs submitted are duly accomplished before submission or attaching them to disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The LGU Management have limited the personnel attendance of technical trainings and hiring JO personnel to handle said works and to send them to trainings in relation to their jobs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require that submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 75-6 and 77-61, to facilitate review and evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption. Likewise, ensure that DTRs submitted are duly accomplished before submission or attaching them to disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management complied the recommendation, however, formats for Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were not detailed particularly the date of travel and the corresponding fuel consumed.
observations.reason: formats for Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were not detailed particularly the date of travel and the corresponding fuel consumed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) cause the refund of the amount of P1,089,200.00 corresponding to the payment of incentive to JO employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Management furnished the Audit Team the computation of the salary differential for JO employees supported by Daily Time Records (DTRs) and contracts. Later refunded some amounts but some were later refunded to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the: ¢ GAD Focal Person to prepare the annual GPB and the corresponding GAD ARs for CY 2016 following the guidelines provided under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW)-National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular No. 2012-1 to ensure that the desired outcomes and goals as identified in the Framework Plan for Women and the goals to institutionalize GAD effort in the government are achieved; and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that at least five percent of the annual appropriation is earmarked for GAD programs, projects and activities for the benefit of the personnel and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Management admitted the deficiency of non-submission of the GPB for CY 2015 but submitted GPB for CY 2016.
observations.reason: observation was reiterated in AAR for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRM) to ensure that all projects chargeable to the LDRRMF are in accordance with the guidelines. Likewise, the Annual Budget be carefully prepared and reviewed by the Municipal Budget Officer so that only appropriate disbursements will be charged against the said fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer complied with the recommendation, however the MDRRM Office justified that their office is not only an office but also being operated as Emergency Operation Center (EOC). The Audit Team advised the Management to communicate their justifications to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) in order to facilitate the approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to: » Adjust the entry made in the General Fund (GF) books at the year-end of 2015 using the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) entry; e Transfer the unutilized LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books at the end of the every year; and e Prepare the above accounting entries only if there is an actual transfer of cash occurred, in accordance with the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Accountant agreed and complied with the recommendation in the AOM No. 16-012. Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 401-16020187, 100-16020353 and 100-16020589 were prepared to correct and facilitate the necessary adjusting entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: e Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other persons responsible in completing the supporting documents to submit the soonest time to the Auditor’s Office the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers; and e Ensure that the required reports and accounts with complete supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Treasurer commented that this was due to the lacking of some Municipal Accountant and other persons responsible in completing the supporting documents to submit the soonest time to the Auditor's Office while only 4 of 9 vouchers for CY 2014 needed to be signed first before transmitted to the accounting. She said that they allow the processing of checks even in the absence of required signatures with initials by their staff for recurring in nature such as assistance, payment of bills and wages. Nonetheless, from the receipt of AOM No. 16-003 relative to this deficiency, her office have started not accepting/processing vouchers without duly signed Obligation Request (OBR) and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert effort to implement/complete the unimplemented/on-going programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund within the year to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator stressed that the delay in the implementation of the projects was due to some administrative issues in the course of implementing projects such as delay in the release of the support fund from National Government Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure that all projects chargeable to the 20% Development Fund are in accordance with the guidelines provided for under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) dated April 13, 2011. Likewise, the Annual Budget be carefully prepared and reviewed by the Municipal Budget Officer so that the actual needs of the Municipality are properly allocated in the budget.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator explained that those expenditures charged to the 20% Development Fund were all related to and/or connected with the implementation of the development programs and projects that contributes to the attainment of desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes. He agreed to comply in the ensuing CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize immediately the capitation fund subsidized by the Philhealth Insurance Corporation and use the said fund for its specific purpose stipulated in the Philhealth Circulars, in compliance with Section 309(b) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Management agreed and promised to comply with the audit recommendations. However, due from NGAs-PHIC account still have huge outstanding balance amounting to $6,197,749.95 as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: e The Municipal Mayor require immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circulars No. 97-002; e Ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and should be strictly implemented; e Stop the granting of cash advances to those with unliquidated cash advances; and e Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted for failure to liquidate cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Municipal Mayor require the immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advances. Some unliquidated cash advance pertains to confidential intelligence fund which were forwarded to COA Central Office for liquidation but no credit memo were issued yet; and the unliquidated cash advance of the deceased Mayor were already forwarded to the Supreme Court for liquidation.
observations.reason: partially unliquidated cash advances pertains to confidential intelligence fund and unliquidated cash advance of the deceased Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) cause the refund of P1,621,000.00 corresponding to the payment of incentive benefits to JO employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The Management of the Municipality did not refund payment of incentive.
observations.reason: No refund for payment of incentive was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Personnel Division, HRMO-Officer-in-Charge, institute measures to ensure that the individuals hired under contracts of service or job orders are not disqualified under CSC Resolution No. 02-0790, as amended. Ensure compliance with CSC laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of contract of services or job orders and on the acknowledgement of the same. Likewise, we recommended that there must be standard rates in the granting of wages of JO personnel.
observations.managementAction: 2014 The Management promised to set a standard rates for each kind of task/work done by a JO employee.
observations.reason: The Management justified that wages were based on the actual work in cognizance of the capability and responsibility on the assigned task.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Treasurer should verify the transactions if it is duly signed by the concerned Head of Unit and approved by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) before payment or issuance of checks made. Likewise, the DVs should be properly acknowledged for the receipt of payment by the payee.
observations.managementAction: 2014 The Management complied with recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations. Likewise, request for technical inspection for completed projects must be submitted to the Commission on Audit. Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 of PD No. 1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of Republic Act No. 6713.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Three out of the seven projects were already submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Engineer justified that the failure to submit the additional documents was due to lack of technical personnel who will prepare the needed documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant should verify the supporting documents attached to the liquidation of travel allowance of employees before recording in the books. Likewise, we recommended that the excess claim should be refunded by the employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: 2014 The Accounting Office is now complying with the recommendation with regard to the supporting documents.
observations.reason: No refund for the excess claim was done by the employees concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure proper computation of RATA in compliance with the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013. We likewise recommend for the refund of the excessive RATA claimed by the Vice Mayor and SB Members.
observations.managementAction: 2013 No refund for the excess RATA was made by the concerned officials. Likewise, this observation and recommendation was reiterated in AAR for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended adherence on the provision of the implementation of 3 tranche for 2nd class municipality and SP Resolution No. 120-2012 Section 1.2. Immediate refunds on the above disallowances should be made by the employees concern.
observations.managementAction: 2012 A Notice of Motion for Reconsideration was issued but pending regarding this matter.
observations.reason: Motion for Reconsideration was pending.

observations.recommendation: We required the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards for all property, plant and equipment especially for buildings of the agency and to compute/ provide depreciation accounting. Exert effort to locate previous records of all purchases for capital outlay to provide date of acquisition and actual cost of properties in the inventory listing/ accounting records and at the same time as basis for the computation of the required depreciation expense.
observations.managementAction: 2012 The Municipal Accountant is now updating her records.
observations.reason: Lacked of adequate records as basis such as PPE Ledger accounts. No personnel were hired to maintain the PPE Ledger Cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: e Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to locate all necessary land documents still in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipality is in the process of reconciliation; e Require the Municipal Accountant to trace/reconstruct records to facilitate reconciliation with the RPCPPE of the Municipal Treasurer as basis in the preparation of PPE Ledger Card; and e Furnish the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant with the complete documents/papers of the above mentioned donations granted to the Supreme Court and Philippine National Police for proper recording purposes in the inventory report and book of accounts.
observations.managementAction: 2012 Some deeds of donation were found by/and already in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipality is in the process of reconciliation.
observations.reason: Lacked of adequate records to present accurate valuation of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU establish restriction/accountability for the proper use and updating of Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (AREs) particularly for officials/employees who already transferred/retired from service. Upon acquisition of equipment and motor vehicles/heavy equipment, see to it that these are properly tag with date of acquisition, cost and correct serial numbers/ engine and chassis numbers in the ARE and RPCPPE in order to prevent from misuse and unauthorized alteration.
observations.managementAction: 2012 Inventory tagging was done by the Management to the concerned offices. Also, the lacking of personnel who will do the job. Likewise, some properties were transferred to the barangays to support the transfer.

observations.recommendation: We demanded that the amount should be immediately deposited to the LGU depository bank corresponding to the unremitted collections. Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Section 32, MNGAS, Volume I. Institute disciplinary measures or criminal action against the erring officials to stop the habitual practice in handling collections.
observations.managementAction: 2011 Mr. Amor Ry Pobre, former Municipal Treasurer, partially paid his unremitted collections to the Municipality amounting to P54,264.98 as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Mr. Pobre still have an outstanding balance of unremitted collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: e Require the concerned Collecting Officer to produce the unaccounted official receipts and submit to the Audit Team for verification to come up with final accountability; e Require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 30, MNGAS, Volume I; and e Institute disciplinary measures or criminal action against the erring officials to stop the habitual failure of accountable officer to render accounts.
observations.managementAction: 2011 The Municipal Treasurer said that Mr. Yanto and Ms. Gonzaga were already deceased while Ms. Abundo and Mr. Pobre were unaccounted forms/OR’s were not yet presented to COA. The Municipal Treasurer said accountable forms/OR's were not yet presented to COA.
observations.reason: The accountable officers were deceased or retired from government service; some accountable forms/OR's were not yet presented to COA.
